---
title: docs()
description: Auto-generated format documentation from combinator metadata.
---

`docs()` generates static documentation for your format by running the parse generator against a synthetic document where every combinator succeeds with a placeholder value.

## Usage

```typescript
const docs = PlanFormat.docs();

console.log(docs.description); // "A project plan..."
console.log(docs.examples); // configured examples
console.log(docs.schema); // JSON schema if provided
console.log(docs.steps); // array of combinator descriptions
```

## DocsStep

Each step has:

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `combinator` | `string` | Name, e.g. `heading(1)` |
| `description` | `string?` | From `.description()` |
| `examples` | `string[]?` | From `.examples()` |

## Adding metadata

You can attach descriptions and examples to yieldables for richer documentation:

```typescript
import { heading, splitByCombinator } from "datamark";

const h1 = heading(1).description("The document title");
const sections = splitByCombinator(heading(2))
  .description("Split into step sections")
  .examples(["## Step", "## Another Step"]);
```

<Callout type="info">
  Metadata fluent methods are available on all yieldables returned by combinators.
</Callout>
